evidence_sentence: Orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) has become the dominant waveform in modern broadband wireless communications, including LTE and 5G NR standards [1].

evidence_sentence: The problem of timing synchronization was first proposed in the seminal work of Schmidl and Cox [2], who introduced a correlation-based method using repeated pilot symbols.

evidence_sentence: [3] later proposed a maximum-likelihood estimator for joint timing and frequency offset recovery, extending the pioneering work of [2] to cyclic-prefix-based detection.
